texto: ...what makes a story unique is not necessarily the information in the story but what the writer chooses to put in or leave out.(pg. 146-147)
explicacao: Citação de Roland Smith sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “...what makes a story unique is not necessarily the information in the story but what the writer …” articula reflexao, writing, story em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: When you do your research write down whatever interests you. Whatever stimulates your imagination. Whatever seems important. A story is built like a stone wall. Not all the stones will fit. Some will have to be discarded. Some broken and reshaped. When you finish the wall it may not look exactly like the wall you envisioned, but it will keep the livestock in and the predators out. (pg. 144)
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Roland Smith: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “When you do your research write down whatever interests you. Whatever stimulates your imagination…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, writing, whatever pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

Not all the stones will fit. Some will have to be discarded. Some broken and reshaped. When you finish the wall it may not look exactly like the wall you envisioned, but it will keep the livestock in and the predators out. (pg. 144)

texto: a good writer should draw the reader in by starting in the middle of the story with a hook, then go back and fill in what happened before the hook. Once you have the reader hooked, you can write whatever you want as you slowly reel them in.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Roland Smith sobre temas ligados a reflexao. A afirmação “a good writer should draw the reader in by starting in the middle of the story with a hook, then …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, advice, writing, útil em momentos de writing.

texto: This is Simba," Nicole said, pointing to the lion. "Is he dangerous?" Asked Chase."Not really. He mauled a trainer, but nothing much.
